rationale: The cited paper (10.1509/jmr.15.0246) is highly aligned with the citing paper’s core construct of religious/God salience effects on negative-response contexts, and the citing note explicitly frames its contribution around a priming attenuation pattern in consumer behavior. Given this conceptual overlap (religious priming moderation mechanisms) and the confirmed DOI match in the reference list, this does not look like an obvious incidental mention.
